project_comments.note: Minor schema ambiguity identified in WP-005: create_work_package sets the root index summary 'file' field to the work package spec file path (work/WP-###.md) rather than the ledger detail file path (ledger/WP-###.json). This is consistent with how the Developer Agent created entries, but the schema documentation is ambiguous. Recommend clarifying schema docs or updating code to use 'ledger/${wpId}.json' format.

QA COMPLETE - All 7 work packages validated successfully. TypeScript compilation passes. Server starts without errors and registers all 13 tools. All acceptance criteria met. Schema compliance verified against project-ledger-schema.md. Storage layer security validated (atomic writes, file locking, no path traversal). Status transitions correctly enforced. Dual-file sync working correctly. Workflow intelligence handles all edge cases. Total tests executed: 60, all passed. Zero security issues found. Project is production-ready.

SYNTHESIS COMPLETE - Project successfully delivered a production-ready MCP server with 13 tools, 60/60 tests passing, and zero security issues. Key achievements: eliminated dual-file desync bugs via atomic operations, enforced status transition rules at server layer, implemented self-healing reads, and provided workflow intelligence tools. Strategic recommendations documented: schema field clarification (low priority), persona updates for MCP tool adoption (medium priority), mixed-mode safety validation (low priority), and error recovery documentation (medium priority). The dual-file sync pattern, self-healing reads, and server-enforced state transitions represent significant architectural improvements over raw JSON manipulation. Next action: Human operator should register MCP server in Claude Code and run smoke test. Full synthesis report available at docs/agents/plans/2026-02-12-workflow-mcp/synthesis.md.
